assertions.description: Describes the KYC-SA portal on swift.com/myswift as the submission mechanism
assertions.rationale: Response states 'The KYC-SA attestation is submitted through the SWIFT MySwift portal at swift.com/myswift' in Step 4.

assertions.description: Explains that an independent assessor is required and lists who qualifies: internal audit (if independent from SWIFT ops), external auditor with SWIFT CSP competency, or SWIFT-certified assessors
assertions.rationale: Response lists all three assessor types: Internal Audit (must be organizationally independent), External Auditor or Cybersecurity Firm (with SWIFT CSP knowledge), and SWIFT-Certified Assessors. Independence requirement is stated.

assertions.description: States the v2026 attestation window (July 1 – December 31, 2026) and consequences of late submission including counterparty notifications
assertions.rationale: Response explicitly states the v2026 window as July 1 – December 31, 2026 in a table, and lists consequences including 'SWIFT notifies your counterparties that you have not attested by the deadline.'

assertions.description: Explains the three attestation statuses for each control: Implemented, Partially Implemented, Not Implemented
assertions.rationale: Response provides the three statuses in a table in Step 4 with clear definitions for each.

assertions.description: Notes that submitted attestations are visible to counterparties who can view compliance status and may request clarification
assertions.rationale: Response in Step 5 states counterparties can see 'Your overall attestation status' and 'Per-control attestation status' and notes 'Requests for clarification on controls marked Partially Implemented or Not Implemented.'
